summary:
- Most congenital heart defects show specific patterns; notably, split S2 is rare in tricuspid atresia due to its unique anatomical structure without normal pulmonary valve function leading to inconsistent auscultation findings typically seen elsewhere.
```
The summary focuses only on the critical aspects related to identifying which finding does NOT characterize tricuspid atresia based primarily around the unusual presence/absence of certain cardiac signs like S2 splitting.
``` ```plaintext
Splitting of the second heart sound (S2) is commonly found in many congenital heart diseases but absent in tricuspid atresia, highlighting an important differential diagnosis point.
Therefore, D stands out as the exception among given characteristics because it doesn't match the standard presentation of tricuspid atresia.
```

This concise version captures the essence while adhering strictly to two sentences focusing solely on clinically pertinent details relative to differentiating between options provided concerning tricuspid atresia.
